steps.title: Let's set up a Q&A slide!
steps.description: A Q&A slide turns any presentation into a two-way conversation — your audience sends questions from their phones and you answer them one by one, live. We'll build one together in under a minute. Click Next to begin.

steps.title: Create a new presentation
steps.description: Click 'New Presentation' to open a fresh deck.

steps.title: Pick the Q&A slide type
steps.description: Select 'Q&A' from the slide type picker. You'll find it in the Interactive section — it's the one that gathers questions from your audience in real time.

steps.title: Write your Q&A heading
steps.description: Type a heading that invites your audience to ask — something clear and welcoming, so participants know exactly what this slide is for.

action.hint: Type your Q&A heading, then click away

steps.title: Choose when the audience can ask
steps.description: Set whether questions can come in on all slides throughout your presentation, or only on this dedicated Q&A slide. Most hosts keep it on 'Only on Q&A slides' to control the timing.

steps.title: Turn on moderation (optional)
steps.description: Check 'Moderate audience's questions' to review and approve questions before they appear on screen — great for large or public events where you need an extra filter.

steps.title: Ready to go live!
steps.description: That's your Q&A slide set up. When you're on — hit 'Present'. Your audience joins on their phones, submits questions, and upvotes their favourites. You organise and answer them all from the same screen.
